title: Golden Globes 2026: Surprises and Upsets
text: The 83rd Golden Globes ceremony delivered several unexpected outcomes that surprised industry observers. Hamnet delivered a shocking upset, winning Best Film Drama over the heavily favored Sinners directed by Ryan Coogler, significantly reshaping the Oscar race conversation. Teyana Taylor secured her first major award of the season for One Battle After Another in supporting actress, defeating Amy Madigan who had been leading in predictions after wins at the New York Film Critics Circle and Critics Choice Awards. Michelle Williams won Best TV Limited/Movie Actress for Dying for Sex despite losing the Emmy and Critics Choice Award, marking her third Golden Globe victory and affirming her status as a perennial Globe favorite. Despite seven nominations and significant momentum, Sinners left largely empty-handed in major categories, winning only the Cinematic and Box Office Achievement award and a non-televised score prize. Jafar Panahi's It Was Just an Accident missed both the Globe and Critics Choice Award for Best Foreign-Language Film, losing to The Secret Agent. Both Wicked: For Good and Avatar: Fire and Ash lost the box office achievement award to Sinners.
